note_data.title: Probability
keyPoints: Natural numbers are all the positive integers from 1 to infinity, fractions or decimals are not included
Whole numbers are all the positive integers including zero
Integers include positive numbers, negative numbers or zero and are the most common word used by the examiner
Prime numbers are numbers that have exactly 2 factors; 1 and the number itself
Composite numbers are numbers that have more than two factors
Rational numbers are any whole number that can be written as a pure fraction or decimal and must either end (terminating decimals) or repeat (recurring decimals)
Irrational numbers are any number that cannot be expressed in the form of a fraction
Squared numbers are always positive and candidates are required to memorise square numbers up till 15 for Paper 1
Cube numbers may be positive or negative

definitions.definition: All the positive integers from 1 to infinity. Fractions or decimals are not included. They are also called Positive Integers
Numbers that have exactly 2 factors; 1 and the number itself
Any whole number, that can be written as a pure fraction or decimal. Numbers that can be expressed in the form of a ratio. They must either end (terminating decimals) or repeat (recurring decimals)
The largest number that is a common factor of two different numbers
The smallest number that is the multiple of two different numbers

howToAnswer.instruction: Show working using probability formulas and give final answer as fraction, decimal or percentage as requested
Use appropriate probability method (tree diagram, formula) and show all steps clearly
Use experimental data to calculate relative frequency
Create tree diagram with all branches labelled with outcomes and probabilities

modelAnswer.question: In Lahore, the probability that it rains on any day is 0.3. Find the probability that it rains on exactly one of two consecutive days.
modelAnswer.answer:
P(rain on day 1) = 0.3, P(no rain on day 1) = 0.7
P(rain on day 2) = 0.3, P(no rain on day 2) = 0.7
P(rain on exactly one day) = P(rain day 1, no rain day 2) + P(no rain day 1, rain day 2)
= (0.3 × 0.7) + (0.7 × 0.3) = 0.21 + 0.21 = 0.42
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Identify probabilities for rain and no rain
Mark 2: Set up correct calculation for exactly one day

commonMistakes: Including fractions or decimals when listing natural numbers
Confusing integers with whole numbers - integers include negative numbers
Not recognizing that squared numbers are always positive while cube numbers may be positive or negative
Repeating the same element in a set when sets are organized, unique collections of elements
examinerTips: Integers are the most common word used by the examiner
Candidates are required to memorise square numbers up till 15 for Paper 1
formulas: P(event) = Number of favourable outcomes ÷ Total number of outcomes
P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B) for independent events
P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) for mutually exclusive events
Experimental probability = Number of successes ÷ Total number of trials
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Tree diagram showing two stages with branches for each outcome, probabilities written on each branch, final outcomes and their probabilities calculated by multiplying along branches
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to find conditional probability P(Y|H') where Georgie wears yellow scarf given she does not wear hat, requiring use of P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B) formula

This topic appeared in O/N 2022 P2 Q7 (4 marks) — students had to find constant k in probability density function f(x) = kx(2-x) for 0≤x≤2, testing integration skills and the fact that total probability equals 1
This was examined in M/J 2023 P1 Q8 (5 marks) — question asked for probability that Nicola obtains 6 for second time on 8th throw, combining geometric and binomial probability concepts
This appeared in O/N 2022 P2 Q5 (7 marks) — students calculated probability that sample mean exceeds 1300 for N(1250, 480²) distribution with n=100, testing normal distribution and sampling theory

workedExample.question: A continuous random variable X has probability density function f(x) = kx(2-x) for 0 ≤ x ≤ 2, and f(x) = 0 elsewhere. Find the value of k. (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: Use the fact that total probability = 1, so ∫₀² kx(2-x) dx = 1
Step 2: Expand the integrand: kx(2-x) = k(2x - x²)
Step 3: Integrate: ∫₀² k(2x - x²) dx = k[x² - x³/3]₀²
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for setting up integral equals 1, 1 mark for correct integration, 1 mark for correct evaluation of limits, 1 mark for final answer k = 3/4

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused P(A|B) with P(A∩B)' — always use the conditional probability formula P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B) and clearly identify which probability is being conditioned on
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot that probability density functions must integrate to 1' — always check your PDF by ensuring ∫f(x)dx = 1 over the entire domain

Examiner Report 2023: 'Candidates often mixed up binomial and geometric distributions in combined probability questions' — clearly identify whether you need probability of exactly r successes (binomial) or probability of first success on trial r (geometric)
note_data.keyDiagram: Venn diagram showing two overlapping circles A and B within a rectangle (sample space S). Mark regions clearly as A∩B (intersection), A∩B' (A only), A'∩B (B only), and A'∩B' (neither). For conditional probability P(A|B), shade only circle B as the new sample space, then highlight A∩B within it. Include probability values in each region and show that P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B) geometrically.
note_data.quickRecap: The number family includes natural numbers (positive integers from 1 to infinity), whole numbers (positive integers including zero), and integers (positive, negative numbers or zero). Prime numbers have exactly 2 factors while composite numbers have more than two factors. Rational numbers can be expressed as fractions and either terminate or repeat as decimals, while irrational numbers cannot be expressed as fractions.
